name: The First National Bank Of Wakefield
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank serving Upper Michigan and Northern Wisconsin since 1910, offering personal and business banking with two local branch locations.
description_long:
First National Bank of Wakefield was organized in 1910 as a private bank by local businessmen in Gogebic County, Michigan, and was chartered as a national bank by the U.S. Treasury Department in February 1919. The bank has remained locally owned and operated for over a century, making it the only locally owned and operated bank in Gogebic County. A second branch was opened in Bessemer in April 2000 to expand service capacity and reach additional communities in the region.

The bank offers personal checking accounts with no service charges, online and mobile banking platforms, ATM access at 16 fee-free locations, personal loans (including specialty financing for ATVs, UTVs, and snowmobiles), and business banking services. They provide FDIC insurance protection and maintain both digital and traditional banking channels, including live customer service representatives available during business hours. Their online banking system was recently updated in October 2021 to provide a modern digital experience.

First National Bank of Wakefield distinguishes itself through genuine community orientation and local decision-making. The bank employs 15 team members with nearly 300 years of combined banking experience (averaging ~20 years per employee). Management explicitly emphasizes personal service—"a real person will answer" when customers call—despite offering state-of-the-art mobile and online banking technology. Directors and shareholders live and work in Gogebic County and are positioned to understand local customer needs.

The bank is well-suited for customers in Upper Michigan and Northern Wisconsin seeking traditional community banking with established local roots and personal service. However, as a regional institution with only two branch locations, it has geographic limitations and is not accessible to customers outside its service area. The lack of detailed information about loan terms, interest rates, and specific product offerings on the website may require direct customer contact for comprehensive product comparisons. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts with no service charges
Specialty loans for ATVs, UTVs, and snowmobiles
Debit/ATM cards with lost or stolen card support
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
No service charge personal checking accounts
16 fee-free ATM locations across service area
Modern mobile banking app with online banking platform
Specialty financing available for ATVs, UTVs, and snowmobiles
Live human representatives answer customer calls (not automated systems)
Over 113 years of continuous local ownership and operation in Gogebic County
Experienced staff averaging ~20 years of banking experience per employee
cons: Only two physical branch locations (Wakefield and Bessemer), limiting accessibility for customers outside Upper Michigan/Northern Wisconsin
Limited digital marketing and information about loan rates, terms, and specific product details on website
Small institution (15 employees) may have less capacity for complex financial products compared to regional/national banks
No mention of credit card offerings, investment services, or wealth management products
Geographic limitation makes services unavailable to customers outside the Upper Peninsula and Northern Wisconsin service area
best_for: Residents of Upper Michigan and Northern Wisconsin seeking a local, community-focused bank
Small business owners and agricultural customers in Gogebic County wanting local decision-making authority
Customers who value speaking with live representatives and personalized service over digital-only banking
People financing recreational vehicles (ATVs, UTVs, snowmobiles) in the region
diagnosis: First National Bank of Wakefield is best for Upper Michigan and Northern Wisconsin residents who prioritize community banking, local decision-making, and personal service from experienced staff. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—the bank only serves Gogebic County and nearby areas through two branches, making it inaccessible for customers outside this region, and the website provides limited detail on competitive rates and terms for key products.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
